info.name: Comprobify API
info.description:
Electronic invoice API for Ecuador's SRI. Set `base_url`, `api_key`, and `admin_secret` in the collection variables before running requests. After creating an invoice, copy the returned `accessKey` into the `access_key` variable to use it across subsequent requests.

Document endpoints require both `Authorization: Bearer <api-key>` and `X-Issuer-Id: <issuer-id>`. Tenant and issuer management require only the Bearer token. Admin endpoints use the `admin_secret` as the Bearer token.

variable.description: Base URL of the API (no trailing slash)
Bearer API key issued for the tenant (from Register or POST /api/keys)
Admin secret (ADMIN_SECRET env var on the server)
49-digit SRI access key — copy from Create Invoice response
Issuer ID — copy from List Issuers or Create Branch response. Used as X-Issuer-Id header on document requests.
API key ID — copy from List Keys response (used for revocation)
Tenant ID — copy from Create Tenant (admin) response
Notification ID — copy from List Notifications response (used for Mark as Read)
Webhook endpoint ID — copy from Register Webhook response

item.name: Registration
item.description: Public registration and email verification endpoints. No authentication required.

formdata.description: Deprecated — ignored. All new accounts start in sandbox. Use POST /api/tenants/promote to move to production.
Optional. Document type codes to enable (default: ["01"])
Optional. Email language: es (default) or en.
Optional. Frontend URL for the email verification link. Token appended as ?token=<token>. https required in production.

request.description:
Self-service registration. Creates a tenant, issuer, and sandbox API key in one call.

Store the returned `apiKey` — it is shown only once. Copy it into the `api_key` collection variable.

If the email already exists and is not suspended, the endpoint revokes the current sandbox key and returns a new one (200 with `recovered: true`).

Optional `verificationRedirectUrl` sets a frontend page for the email verification link.
Verify your email address using the token from the registration email. Required before promoting to production.

Resends the verification email. Generates a fresh token (invalidating the previous one) and resets the expiry.

Two rate limits apply:
- IP-based: shared with POST /api/register — 5 requests per hour per IP.
- Per-account cooldown: 60 seconds between resends for the same email. Returns 429 if not elapsed.

Returns the same generic message whether or not the email exists (no enumeration).

item.name: Tenants
item.description: Tenant settings and lifecycle management. Require a Bearer API key.

request.description: Updates the preferred language for the authenticated tenant. Used for all outgoing emails. Supported values: `es` (Spanish, default), `en` (English).

Promotes the entire tenant (all branches) from sandbox to production. This is **one-way** — cannot be reversed.

All active sandbox API keys are revoked automatically. The response includes one new production key per revoked sandbox key (same label). **Store all tokens immediately — they are shown only once.**

`initialSequentials` is optional. If omitted, all active document types on all issuers start at sequential 1. Pass `issuerId` + `documentType` + `sequential` for each combination you want to pre-seed.

Requires the tenant's email to be verified (status ACTIVE).

item.name: API Keys
item.description: Tenant-facing API key management. Mint named keys for each integration, list them, and revoke leaked or unused ones. Require a Bearer API key.

request.description:
Returns every active key for the tenant. The plaintext token is **never** returned — only labels, environments, ids, and creation dates.

Copy a key's `id` into the `api_key_id` variable to use it with Revoke Key.

Creates a new tenant-scoped API key. The plaintext token is shown **once** in the response — store it immediately.

`label` (optional, max 100 chars) — a human-readable name for the integration, e.g. `frontend-prod`, `erp`, `mobile-app`.

`environment` — `"sandbox"` (default) or `"production"`. Production keys can only be minted after the tenant has been promoted to production.

When used on a document request, the key's environment must match the targeted issuer's environment — otherwise the request is rejected with 401.

Marks the key as inactive. The key stops working immediately and cannot be used to authenticate any future request.

You cannot revoke the key you are currently using to make this request — use a different key, or contact admin support.

item.name: Issuers
item.description: Issuer (branch / issue point) management. Require a Bearer API key.

request.description:
Returns all active issuers (branches / issue points) belonging to the authenticated tenant.

Copy an issuer's `id` into the `issuer_id` collection variable to use it as `X-Issuer-Id` on document requests.
Returns profile information for a single issuer owned by the authenticated tenant: RUC, business name, trade name, branch/issue point codes, and certificate expiry/fingerprint.

formdata.description: Optional. Document type codes to enable (default: ["01"])
Optional. Numeric id of the issuer to inherit cert/profile from. Defaults to the tenant's first existing issuer. Ignored if a cert file is uploaded.
Optional — leave blank to reuse the calling issuer certificate

request.description:
Creates a new branch or issue point for the tenant. Inherits RUC, business name, and certificate from an existing issuer of the tenant.

**No new API key is minted** — your existing tenant key already covers every branch via the `X-Issuer-Id` header. New branches inherit the tenant's current environment (sandbox or production).

The cert file and certPassword fields are optional — only provide them if the new branch needs a different certificate.

Tier limits apply: FREE allows 1 branch / 1 issue point, STARTER 3 / 2, GROWTH 10 / 5, BUSINESS unlimited.
Returns the active document types enabled for the specified issuer.
Enables a document type for the specified issuer. If the type was previously removed, it is reactivated. Returns the full updated list of active document types.
Disables a document type for the specified issuer. The last active type cannot be removed.

item.name: Documents
item.description: Document lifecycle endpoints. All requests require a Bearer API key and the `X-Issuer-Id` header identifying the issuing branch.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)
Optional — unique per intended invoice. Reuse on retries to avoid duplicates.

request.description:
Creates, XSD-validates, and signs a new electronic invoice. Returns the document with status `SIGNED`.

After a successful response, copy the `accessKey` value into the `access_key` collection variable.

The `X-Issuer-Id` header identifies which branch and certificate to use. Get the id from `GET /api/issuers`.

The `Idempotency-Key` header makes creation idempotent — generate it once per intended invoice and reuse on retries.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

query.description: Filter by status: SIGNED, RECEIVED, RETURNED, AUTHORIZED, NOT_AUTHORIZED

request.description: Lists documents for the specified issuer with optional filtering and pagination. Returns documents sorted by creation date (newest first).

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

request.description: Retrieves document metadata by access key. Does not call the SRI — use Check Authorization for status updates.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

request.description: Submits the signed XML to the SRI reception service. Document moves to `RECEIVED` or `RETURNED`.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

request.description: Queries the SRI authorization service. Document moves to `AUTHORIZED` or `NOT_AUTHORIZED`. On authorization, an email with the RIDE PDF and XML is sent to the buyer.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

request.description: Corrects and re-signs a document in `RETURNED` or `NOT_AUTHORIZED` status. Keeps the original `accessKey`, `sequential`, and `issueDate`. Send again after rebuilding.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

request.description: Downloads the RIDE PDF for an `AUTHORIZED` document. Returns `application/pdf`.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

request.description: Downloads the authorization XML for an `AUTHORIZED` document, or the signed XML for earlier statuses. Returns `application/xml`.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

request.description: Returns the full audit trail for the document — every lifecycle transition, SRI call, and email event.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

query.description: Set to true to resend an email that was already successfully delivered

request.description: Retries the authorization email for a single document. Document must be `AUTHORIZED` with `email_status` of `PENDING` or `FAILED`. Add `?force=true` to resend an already-delivered email.

header.description: Numeric id of the issuing branch (from GET /api/issuers)

request.description: Retries all `PENDING` and `FAILED` authorization emails across all documents for the specified issuer. Maximum 100 per call.

item.name: Catalogs
item.description: Reference data for SRI-defined codes. Use these to build valid invoice requests. Require a Bearer API key.

request.description: Returns buyer identification types (e.g. 04 = RUC, 05 = Cédula, 06 = Pasaporte). Use the `code` as `buyer.idType` on invoice creation.
Returns SRI payment method codes. Use the `code` as `payments[].method` on invoice creation.
Returns accepted SRI payment term units (e.g. dias, meses). Use the `code` as `payments[].termUnit` (with `payments[].term`) when sending invoices a plazos.
Returns SRI tax type codes (e.g. 2 = IVA, 3 = ICE, 5 = IRBPNR). Use the `code` as `taxes[].code` on invoice items.
Returns tax rate codes grouped by tax type. Use `taxCode` as `taxes[].code` and `rateCode` as `taxes[].rateCode` on invoice items.

item.name: Notifications
item.description:
Tenant-level alerts. Both read and unread are returned; use `readAt` to decide what to show. Use `?sinceId=<id>` for efficient catch-up polling — pass the highest id seen on the last request to receive only new notifications.

All requests require a Bearer API key.

header.description: Optional — filter to a specific issuer's notifications plus tenant-level ones. Omit for all-tenant view.

query.description: Optional — return only notifications with id > sinceId. Store the highest id seen and pass it on the next poll to catch up efficiently.

request.description:
Returns active (unexpired) notifications for the tenant, newest first. Both read and unread are included.

**Polling pattern:** call on a schedule (e.g. every 60 s). Use `?sinceId=<id>` to retrieve only notifications created after your last poll — store the highest `id` you've seen and pass it on the next request.

Optional `X-Issuer-Id` header restricts results to a specific issuer's notifications plus tenant-level ones (`issuerId: null`). Omit it for an all-tenant view.

Marks a notification as read at the tenant level. Call this only when every user with access to the notification has acknowledged it on the frontend side. After this call, the notification no longer contributes to `unreadCount` on future polls.

Copy a notification `id` from the List Notifications response into the `notification_id` variable.
Returns the full notification preference list for the tenant — one entry per notification type. Types the tenant has never explicitly configured default to `enabled: true` (opt-out model).

Bulk-upsert notification preferences for the tenant. Send only the types you want to change — unmentioned types are unchanged.

When `enabled` is `false` for a type, no new notifications of that type are created. Existing unread notifications remain and can still be marked as read.

Valid types: `DOCUMENT_AUTHORIZED`, `CERT_EXPIRING`, `CERT_EXPIRED`, `SRI_SUBMISSION_FAILED`, `EMAIL_DELIVERY_FAILED`, `QUOTA_WARNING`.

item.name: Webhooks
item.description:
Register HTTPS callback URLs to receive event notifications in near-real time. When a notification is created or updated, the API immediately POSTs a signed payload to every subscribed active endpoint.

**Secret handling:** the signing secret is returned only at registration. Store it immediately — it cannot be retrieved later. If lost, deregister and re-register the endpoint.

**Tier limits:** FREE: 1 endpoint, STARTER: 2, GROWTH: 5, BUSINESS: 10.

All requests require a Bearer API key.

request.description:
Registers a new webhook endpoint and returns the signing `secret` — **shown only once**. Store it immediately; it is used to verify `X-Comprobify-Signature` on incoming webhook requests.

`url` must be a valid HTTPS URL.

`eventTypes` is optional. Omit it or pass `[]` to subscribe to **all** event types. Valid values: `DOCUMENT_AUTHORIZED`, `CERT_EXPIRING`, `CERT_EXPIRED`, `SRI_SUBMISSION_FAILED`, `EMAIL_DELIVERY_FAILED`, `QUOTA_WARNING`.

Copy the returned `id` into the `webhook_id` collection variable.
Returns all active webhook endpoints for the tenant. Signing secrets are never included in list responses.

Updates a webhook endpoint's URL, event subscriptions, or active flag. All fields are optional — send only what you want to change.

Copy an endpoint `id` from the List Webhooks response into the `webhook_id` variable.

Soft-deletes a webhook endpoint (`active = false`). The endpoint stops receiving deliveries immediately. Past delivery records are preserved for audit purposes.

Copy an endpoint `id` from the List Webhooks response into the `webhook_id` variable.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Admin endpoints for managing tenants, issuers, and API keys. Require the `ADMIN_SECRET` as a Bearer token.

request.description:
Create a tenant for manual onboarding. Status is set to ACTIVE automatically — no email verification required.

Copy the returned `id` into the `tenant_id` collection variable.
Activate or suspend a tenant. Accepted values: `ACTIVE`, `SUSPENDED`.
Manually activate a tenant's email when email delivery is not configured or for support purposes.

Admin override for tenant promotion — skips the ACTIVE status check (does not require verified email). One-way action.

`initialSequentials` is optional. If omitted, all active document types on all issuers start at sequential 1.

All sandbox API keys are revoked and replaced with production keys. The response includes all new production tokens — store them immediately.

Generates a new tenant-scoped API key. The plaintext token is returned only once — store it immediately.

`label` is optional (max 100 chars). `environment` is `"sandbox"` (default) or `"production"`.

Copy the returned key value into the `api_key` collection variable.

formdata.description: Branch address (optional — defaults to mainAddress if omitted)
true if the issuer is required to keep accounting (obligado a llevar contabilidad)
Optional. Document type codes to enable (default: ["01"])
Optional — pre-seed sequential counters. Example: [{"documentType":"01","sequential":500}] means the first invoice will be 000000500.

request.description:
Creates a new issuer for a tenant by uploading a P12 certificate. Requires `tenantId`. **Does NOT return an API key** — mint one separately via `POST /api/admin/tenants/:id/api-keys`.

After a successful response, copy the issuer `id` into `issuer_id`.

formdata.description: ID of the issuer to copy the certificate from
Branch address (optional — defaults to mainAddress if omitted)
Different branch code from the source issuer
true if the issuer is required to keep accounting

request.description: Creates a new branch issuer by copying the certificate from an existing issuer. No P12 file needed. Requires `tenantId`. **Does NOT return an API key** — mint one separately via `POST /api/admin/tenants/:id/api-keys`.
Returns all active issuers across all tenants.
Revokes (soft-deletes) an API key by ID. The key stops working immediately.

Runs all periodic notification jobs across every non-suspended tenant:

1. **Certificate expiry checks** — inspects `cert_expiry` for every active issuer and upserts `CERT_EXPIRING` / `CERT_EXPIRED` alerts. Auto-dismisses alerts when a cert has been renewed (> 30 days remaining).

2. **Webhook retry queue** — retries all `RETRYING` webhook deliveries past their scheduled `next_retry_at` time.

This endpoint is designed to be called by an external scheduler (cron, infrastructure job) on a regular interval — every 5 minutes is a reasonable default. The job is **idempotent**: running it multiple times is safe.

Response includes `tenantsChecked` and a `retries` breakdown (`attempted`, `succeeded`, `failed`, `exhausted`).
Returns `{ status: "ok", uptime }` (200) when the database is reachable, or `{ status: "error", uptime }` (503) when it is not. Use for load balancer health checks and container liveness probes.
